This family consists of approximately 170 genera and over 5,000 species, many of which are native to the tropical regions of the world. The genus Miconia itself is one of the largest genera within the Melastomataceae family, containing numerous species found in the Americas, from Mexico to Argentina. Research has identified various health benefits associated with Miconia josecuatrecasasii, including its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its use in treating wounds, infections, and other health conditions.
